sample.assertion: Every AI system ever built…works entirely with numbers at its core
binary — a way of writing any number using only two digits: 0 and 1
One second of standard-quality audio contains roughly 44,100 numbers
the letter 'A' is conventionally stored as the number 65
richer conversion for words and context covered in topics 6.7 and 6.8
parameters, also known as weights; an LLM may have billions of parameters
the deeper meaning story spans topics 6.5 through 6.9
Two different representations…wildly more or less accurate
Spam filters convert email text into numbers and compare to past spam
Do not confuse the representation with the thing itself

faithfulness.notes: All sampled assertions trace to the corrected corpus. The forward-ref numbers in the reading (6.7/6.8, 6.5–6.9, start 6.5) match the corpus exactly (corpus lines 62, 90, 111). No reading↔corpus drift.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms defined on first use; LLM spelled out. Short sentences, active voice, no academic hedging. Concrete-first throughout.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds ~4 sentences. Worked Example uses a numbered 6-step list; data-type conversions and In-Practice products use bullet lists; In-Practice principles use a table. Inline examples accompany concept introductions. Bold-term/plain-meaning shape used for definitions.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights anywhere in reading.md (cleaner than the 3-4 gold reading, which carries a flagged A1/due-W3/10% line).

soft_observations.observation: In Practice (lines 67–68) uses plain-English 'most similar'/'closest' to describe matching; gestures at similarity (6.5/6.9 territory) without defining or teaching the mechanism. No prohibited terms; named-and-defer not violated.

scope.notes: Reading stays on the headline. Deferred downstream concepts (embeddings, vector-as-point, king-man arithmetic, cosine) appear only as bare mention-and-defer pointers with correct topic numbers — never bolded, never defined.
